questions.question: A cylinder has radius 4 cm and height 10 cm. A cone with the same base radius and height is carved out from inside. What is the volume of the remaining solid in cubic centimeters?
questions.explanation: Volume of cylinder = πr²h = π(4²)(10) = 160π. Volume of cone = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3)π(4²)(10) = (160π)/3. Remaining = 160π − 160π/3 = (480π − 160π)/3 = 320π.

questions.question: A sphere has surface area 144π square inches. What is the radius of the sphere?
questions.explanation: Surface area of sphere = 4πr². Set 4πr² = 144π, so r² = 36, giving r = 6.

questions.question: A rectangular prism has dimensions 5 cm × 8 cm × h cm. If its volume is 280 cubic centimeters, what is h?
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height, so 5 × 8 × h = 280, giving 40h = 280, thus h = 7.

questions.question: A cone has volume 150π cubic inches. If its height is 18 inches, what is the radius of its base?
questions.explanation: Volume of cone = (1/3)πr²h. Substitute: 150π = (1/3)πr²(18) = 6πr². So r² = 25, giving r = 5.

questions.question: Two spheres have radii in the ratio 2:3. What is the ratio of their volumes?
questions.explanation: Volume of sphere = (4/3)πr³. If radii are in ratio 2:3, then volumes are in ratio (2³):(3³) = 8:27 (volume scales with the cube of radius).

questions.question: A cylinder with radius 3 m and height 12 m is completely filled with water. If 60% of the water is drained, how much water remains in cubic meters?
questions.explanation: Initial volume = πr²h = π(3²)(12) = 108π. If 60% is drained, 40% remains. Remaining = 0.40 × 108π = 43.2π = 54π/2.5 ≈ 54π cubic meters. (Correction: 40% of 108π = 43.2π ≈ 135.8 m³, or leave as 43.2π.)
